channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Karnataka Doctor Killed in Domestic Violence Incident; Wife in Custody
item.description: A 45-year-old anaesthetist, Dr Kiran Honnannavar, was found stabbed to death in his Dharwad apartment, with his eight-year-old son suffering severe injuries. Police suspect domestic violence, as the w...

item.title: US and Iran Escalate Military Tensions in Gulf Region
item.description: The United States launched a second wave of attacks on Iran's coastal defenses and missile sites within 24 hours of reimposing a naval blockade of Iranian ports. Tehran retaliated by targeting US mili...

item.title: Legal Dispute Over Alleged Workplace Threats and Contract Termination
item.description: A legal battle has erupted between former KIIS FM radio host Jackie 'O' Henderson and her former employer ARN Media, with the company alleging she threatened a workplace harassment complaint against c...

item.title: Greek islands declare drought emergencies as climate change strains water supplies
item.description: Seven Greek islands in the Aegean Sea have declared drought emergencies to conserve water as climate change intensifies summer heat and disrupts rainfall patterns. The crisis is particularly severe on...

item.title: Uganda Police Arrest Man Accused of Pretending to Be Female Domestic Worker to Steal from Employers
item.description: A man named Kayaga Rayan was arrested in Uganda after being caught posing as a female domestic worker to steal from his employers. The police in Nateete identified him as the suspect, alleging that he...

item.title: Goose App Faces Criticism Over Moderation and Inclusivity Issues
item.description: The article discusses the controversy surrounding the new gay dating app Goose, which positions itself as a less-hookup-focused alternative to Grindr. While the app aims to create a more genuine socia...

item.title: Supreme Court to Review Stay on 'Mahaprabhu Jagannath' Film
item.description: The Supreme Court of India has agreed to hear a plea challenging the Orissa High Court's order that stayed the release of the animated film 'Mahaprabhu Jagannath'. The film, originally scheduled to pr...

item.title: Geopolitical tensions and low inventories sustain Brent crude above $80, fueling inflation concerns
item.description: Brent crude prices remain above $80 per barrel, driven by geopolitical uncertainties and constrained global energy supplies. Analysts warn that the combination of low inventories and ongoing Middle Ea...

item.title: Texas Faces Severe Flooding as Guadalupe River Overflows
item.description: A catastrophic flood wave is currently threatening the Texas Hill Country, with the Guadalupe River surging to levels comparable to the deadly July 4, 2025 floods. The National Weather Service has iss...

item.title: Key Developments in African Affairs: New Species, Legal Rulings, and Regional Cooperation
item.description: A new species of monkey, the Colobus congoensis, was discovered in the Democratic Republic of Congo, highlighting biodiversity challenges. In Kenya, a court rejected Rastafarians' bid to use cannabis ...

item.title: Lil Wayne's Former Assistant Details Unusual Job Requirements Including Hygiene and Dog Care
item.description: In legal documents obtained by TMZ, Lil Wayne's former assistant Andrew Williams revealed the bizarre and specific duties required for his role as the rapper's personal aide. The job description, now ...

item.title: Ontario Premier Doug Ford addresses controversy over MPP hotel expense claims
item.description: Premier Doug Ford of Ontario has responded to ongoing controversy surrounding hotel expense claims by members of his provincial legislature. The scandal involves multiple Progressive Conservative MPPs...

item.title: Marcos authorizes AFP and PNP to ensure peaceful BARMM elections
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. has authorized the involvement of the Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) and Philippine National Police (PNP) to ensure the peaceful conduct of the Bangsamoro Autonom...

item.title: England's World Cup exit sparks criticism of Tuchel's defensive tactics
item.description: England's World Cup exit has led to heavy criticism of head coach Thomas Tuchel for his defensive tactics. The German coach, brought in to fix England's tactical issues, faced backlash after his team ...

item.title: Republican Panic Over Trump's Unpredictable Speech in Election Year
item.description: The article discusses the growing anxiety among Republican operatives regarding President Donald Trump's planned prime-time speech, which they fear could derail their electoral prospects in the 2026 m...

item.title: Comparative analysis of anti-corruption strategies in Kenya and Uganda
item.description: The article compares Kenya and Uganda's approaches to combating corruption, highlighting Kenya's focus on civil asset recovery and Uganda's emphasis on high-profile takedowns. Kenya's Ethics and Anti-...

item.title: DOH reaffirms deworming safety amid student death investigation
item.description: The Department of Health (DOH) has clarified that the deworming medication Albendazole remains safe and effective for children, urging the public to avoid spreading misinformation following the tragic...

item.title: Teenagers' Views on UK Social Media Curfew and Government Proposals
item.description: The article explores the mixed reactions of 16- and 17-year-olds in the UK to the government's proposed social media curfew, which would enforce a midnight to 6am block on certain apps. While the poli...

item.title: Karachi court grants 7-day physical remand to three suspects in Dr Akash murder case
item.description: A judicial magistrate in Karachi approved a seven-day physical remand for three suspects arrested in the murder of Dr Akash Kumar, a young doctor killed during a robbery near Teen Talwar. The incident...

item.title: India Halts Seafarer Deployments Amid Hormuz Attacks and Rising Tensions with Iran
item.description: The Indian government has directed shipping companies to avoid deploying Indian seafarers on vessels traversing the Strait of Hormuz due to heightened security risks following a series of attacks on c...
